Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum of Fibonacci numbers up to F(n) (inclusive).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FibSum
  (defun fsum (n)
    (def a 0) (def b 1) (def s 0) (def i 0)
    (while (<= i n)
      (do
        (set s (+ s a))
        (def t (+ a b))
        (set a b)
        (set b t)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    s)
  (test n0 (eq? (fsum 0) 0))
  (test n5 (eq? (fsum 5) 12))
  (test n10 (eq? (fsum 10) 143)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write n copies of 'x' to path and return the write status.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module WriteNX
  (extern defun file_write ((path : Str) (body : Str)) : Num @capability "file.write")
  (defun build_x (n)
    (def s "")
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (set s (str_concat s "x"))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    s)
  (defun write_xs (path n)
    (file_write path (build_x n)))
  (test small (mocks (file.write "/t" 1)) (assert-eq (write_xs "/t" 3) 1))
  (test zero (mocks (file.write "/t" 1)) (assert-eq (write_xs "/t" 0) 1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 2 * length of the file's content.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FileByteDbl
  (extern defun file_read (p) @capability "file.read")
  (defun bdbl (p)
    (* 2 (str_length (file_read p))))
  (test empty (mocks (file.read "/a" "")) (eq? (bdbl "/a") 0))
  (test small (mocks (file.read "/a" "hi")) (eq? (bdbl "/a") 4))
  (test big (mocks (file.read "/a" "abcdefghij")) (eq? (bdbl "/a") 20)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return the second-largest value in an array of distinct integers.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SecondMax
  (defun smax (a n)
    (def m1 (arr.get a 0))
    (def m2 -1000000000)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def v (arr.get a i))
        (if (> v m1) (do (set m2 m1) (set m1 v))
                     (if (> v m2) (set m2 v) 0))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    m2)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 3) (arr.set a 1 8) (arr.set a 2 1) (arr.set a 3 9) (arr.set a 4 4)
    (assert-eq (smax a 5) 8))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Linearly interpolate between a and b at parameter t in [0,1].

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Lerp
  (defun lerp (a b t)
    (+ a (* t (- b a))))
  (test zero (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 0.0) 10.0 0.0001))
  (test one (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 1.0) 20.0 0.0001))
  (test half (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 0.5) 15.0 0.0001))
  (test quarter (near? (lerp 0.0 100.0 0.25) 25.0 0.0001)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

P*(1+r/n)^(n*t), with rounding.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module CompoundQ
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun rate_per (annual n) (/ (/ annual 100) n))
  (defun grow (p r n t) (* p (math_pow (+ 1 r) (* n t))))
  (defun amount (p annual n t)
    (def r (rate_per annual n))
    (round2 (grow p r n t)))
  (test no (near? (amount 1000 0 4 5) 1000.0 0.01))
  (test annual (near? (amount 1000 5 1 1) 1050.0 0.05))
  (test quarterly (near? (amount 1000 5 4 5) 1282.04 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Compute Euclidean norm, then divide each component (unit-norm check).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Normalize
  (defun norm (x y z)
    (math.sqrt (+ (+ (* x x) (* y y)) (* z z))))
  (defun unit_x (x y z) (/ x (norm x y z)))
  (test axis (near? (unit_x 3.0 0.0 0.0) 1.0 0.0001))
  (test diag (near? (unit_x 1.0 1.0 0.0) 0.7071067811 0.0001)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper water: tier1_use, tier2_use, tier3_use, tax, total + round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Water5
  (defun min2 (a b)
    (if (< a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun max2 (a b)
    (if (> a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun tier1 (gallons)
    (* (min2 gallons 1000) 0.005))
  (defun tier2 (gallons)
    (* (max2 0 (- (min2 gallons 5000) 1000)) 0.010))
  (defun tier3 (gallons)
    (* (max2 0 (- gallons 5000)) 0.020))
  (defun apply_tax (amt pct)
    (* amt (+ 1 (/ pct 100))))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun bill (gallons tax_pct)
    (def sub (+ (tier1 gallons) (+ (tier2 gallons) (tier3 gallons))))
    (round2 (apply_tax sub tax_pct)))
  (test small (near? (bill 500 0) 2.5 0.01))
  (test mid (near? (bill 3000 0) 25.0 0.01))
  (test high (near? (bill 7000 0) 85.0 0.01))
  (test taxed (near? (bill 3000 8) 27.0 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return smaller of two numbers.

(module Min2
  (defun my_min (a b)
    (if (< a b) (return a))
    b)
  (test asc (eq? (my_min 1 2) 1))
  (test desc (eq? (my_min 5 3) 3))
  (test eq (eq? (my_min 4 4) 4))
  (test negs (eq? (my_min -3 -7) -7)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Subtract mean from each, divide by stddev; check first element approx.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Zscore
  (defun mean (a n)
    (def s 0.0) (def i 0)
    (while (< i n) (do (set s (+ s (arr.get a i))) (set i (+ i 1))))
    (/ s n))
  (defun stddev (a n mu)
    (def s 0.0) (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def d (- (arr.get a i) mu))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (math.sqrt (/ s n)))
  (defun z_first (a n)
    (def mu (mean a n))
    (def sd (stddev a n mu))
    (/ (- (arr.get a 0) mu) sd))
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 2.0) (arr.set a 1 4.0) (arr.set a 2 4.0) (arr.set a 3 4.0) (arr.set a 4 6.0)
    (assert-near (z_first a 5) -1.5811388 0.0001))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Standard loan formula with helpers: monthly_rate, total_periods, growth, payment, round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module LoanFull
  (defun monthly_rate (annual_pct)
    (/ (/ annual_pct 100) 12))
  (defun total_periods (years)
    (* years 12))
  (defun growth (r n)
    (math_pow (+ 1 r) n))
  (defun amortize (p r n)
    (def g (growth r n))
    (/ (* p (* r g)) (- g 1)))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun monthly_payment (principal annual_pct years)
    (def r (monthly_rate annual_pct))
    (def n (total_periods years))
    (if (= r 0) (return (round2 (/ principal n))))
    (round2 (amortize principal r n)))
  (test zero_rate (near? (monthly_payment 12000 0 1) 1000.0 0.05))
  (test mortgage (near? (monthly_payment 200000 4.5 30) 1013.37 0.05))
  (test car (near? (monthly_payment 25000 6 5) 483.32 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Longest run of same character in string.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module LongRun
  (defun longest_run (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def best 1)
    (def cur 1)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) (str_substring s (- i 1) 1))
        (set cur (+ cur 1))
        (set cur 1))
      (if (> cur best) (set best cur))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    best)
  (test empty (eq? (longest_run "") 0))
  (test single (eq? (longest_run "a") 1))
  (test simple (eq? (longest_run "aabbb") 3))
  (test alt (eq? (longest_run "abab") 1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Session: per-hour + group discount + materials.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Tutor
  (defun base_rate (hours rate)
    (* hours rate))
  (defun group_discount (cost students)
    (if (>= students 4) (return (* cost 0.7)))
    (if (>= students 2) (return (* cost 0.85)))
    cost)
  (defun materials_fee (count)
    (* count 5))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun total (hours rate students materials)
    (round2 (+ (group_discount (base_rate hours rate) students) (materials_fee materials))))
  (test solo (near? (total 1 50 1 0) 50.0 0.01))
  (test pair (near? (total 1 50 2 0) 42.5 0.01))
  (test group (near? (total 1 50 4 0) 35.0 0.01))
  (test with_mat (near? (total 1 50 1 2) 60.0 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Check whether s ends with suffix x.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module EndsWith
  (defun ends (s x)
    (def ns (str_length s))
    (def nx (str_length x))
    (if (< ns nx) (return 0))
    (if (str_eq (str_substring s (- ns nx) nx) x) 1 0))
  (test yes (eq? (ends "report.txt" ".txt") 1))
  (test no (eq? (ends "report.txt" ".log") 0))
  (test exact (eq? (ends "abc" "abc") 1))
  (test tooLong (eq? (ends "x" "abc") 0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Read A,B,C env, join with '/'.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module EnvJoin3
  (extern defun env_read (k) @capability "env.get")
  (defun join3 ()
    (def a (env_read "A"))
    (def b (env_read "B"))
    (def c (env_read "C"))
    (str_concat a (str_concat "/" (str_concat b (str_concat "/" c)))))
  (test all (mocks (env.get "A" "x") (env.get "B" "y") (env.get "C" "z")) (eq? (join3) "x/y/z")))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper boarding: base_per_night, food_supplement, grooming, vet_visit, total + round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Pet5
  (defun base_per_night (size_kg nights)
    (def base 30)
    (if (> size_kg 25) (set base 45))
    (* base nights))
  (defun food_supplement (special_diet days)
    (if (= special_diet 1) (return (* days 8)))
    0)
  (defun grooming (count cost_each)
    (* count cost_each))
  (defun vet_visit (need_vet)
    (if (= need_vet 1) (return 75))
    0)
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun total (size_kg nights special_diet groom_count groom_cost vet)
    (round2 (+ (base_per_night size_kg nights)
               (+ (food_supplement special_diet nights)
                  (+ (grooming groom_count groom_cost) (vet_visit vet))))))
  (test small_simple (near? (total 5 3 0 0 0 0) 90.0 0.01))
  (test large (near? (total 30 3 0 0 0 0) 135.0 0.01))
  (test full (near? (total 30 5 1 2 50 1) 440.0 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Build running max array.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module RunMax
  (defun running_max (xs n)
    (def out (arr_new n))
    (if (= n 0) (return out))
    (arr_set out 0 (arr_get xs 0))
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (def cur (arr_get xs i))
      (def prev (arr_get out (- i 1)))
      (if (> cur prev)
        (arr_set out i cur)
        (arr_set out i prev))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    out)
  (test simple
    (def a (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 3) (arr_set a 2 2) (arr_set a 3 5)
    (def r (running_max a 4))
    (eq? (arr_get r 3) 5)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Items*price + box fee + shipping zone.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Warehouse
  (defun items_total (qty price)
    (* qty price))
  (defun box_fee (qty)
    (if (<= qty 10) (return 1))
    (if (<= qty 50) (return 3))
    5)
  (defun zone_ship (zone)
    (if (= zone 0) (return 5))
    (if (= zone 1) (return 10))
    20)
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun order_total (qty price zone)
    (round2 (+ (items_total qty price) (+ (box_fee qty) (zone_ship zone)))))
  (test small (near? (order_total 5 10 0) 56.0 0.01))
  (test medium (near? (order_total 30 10 1) 313.0 0.01))
  (test large (near? (order_total 100 5 2) 525.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Read env var FEATURE_X and treat 'on'/'1'/'true' as enabled (returning 1) else 0.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FeatFlag
  (extern defun env_read ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")
  (defun is_enabled ()
    (def v (str_lower (env_read "FEATURE_X")))
    (if (str_eq v "on") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq v "1") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq v "true") (return 1))
    0)
  (test on (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "on")) (assert-eq (is_enabled) 1))
  (test true (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true")) (assert-eq (is_enabled) 1))
  (test off (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "off")) (assert-eq (is_enabled) 0))
  (test missing (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "")) (assert-eq (is_enabled) 0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Increment a counter under key k, initializing it to 1 if missing.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module MapIncOrInit
  (defun bump (m k)
    (if (map.has m k)
      (map.set m k (+ (map.get m k) 1))
      (map.set m k 1))
    m)
  (test t (do
    (def m : (Map Str Num) (map.new))
    (bump m "x")
    (bump m "x")
    (bump m "y")
    (assert-eq (map.get m "x") 2)
    (assert-eq (map.get m "y") 1))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Cleaning + X-rays + filling + insurance.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Dental
  (defun cleaning_fee () 100)
  (defun xray_fee (count) (* count 50))
  (defun filling_fee (count) (* count 200))
  (defun apply_insurance (cost coverage_pct)
    (* cost (- 1 (/ coverage_pct 100))))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun out_of_pocket (xrays fillings coverage)
    (def total (+ (cleaning_fee) (+ (xray_fee xrays) (filling_fee fillings))))
    (round2 (apply_insurance total coverage)))
  (test cleaning_only (near? (out_of_pocket 0 0 0) 100.0 0.01))
  (test full_visit (near? (out_of_pocket 2 1 0) 400.0 0.01))
  (test with_insurance (near? (out_of_pocket 2 1 50) 200.0 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Three-tier rates: 5% up to 1000, 10% on 1000–5000, 20% above 5000. Round to 2dp.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ThreeBracket
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))
  (defun tier (x)
    (def t1 (* (math.min x 1000.0) 0.05))
    (def t2 (* (math.max 0.0 (- (math.min x 5000.0) 1000.0)) 0.10))
    (def t3 (* (math.max 0.0 (- x 5000.0)) 0.20))
    (round2 (+ (+ t1 t2) t3)))
  (test zero (near? (tier 0.0) 0.0 0.01))
  (test in_first (near? (tier 500.0) 25.0 0.01))
  (test boundary1 (near? (tier 1000.0) 50.0 0.01))
  (test in_second (near? (tier 3000.0) 250.0 0.01))
  (test boundary2 (near? (tier 5000.0) 450.0 0.01))
  (test in_third (near? (tier 10000.0) 1450.0 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Count lines: each '\n' starts a new line; trailing non-empty content also counts as a line.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module LineCount
  (extern defun file_read ((path : Str)) : Str @capability "file.read")
  (defun line_count (path)
    (def s (file_read path))
    (def n (str_length s))
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def c 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (if (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) "\n") (set c (+ c 1)) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (if (str_eq (str_substring s (- n 1) 1) "\n") c (+ c 1)))
  (test empty (mocks (file.read "/f" "")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 0))
  (test one_terminated (mocks (file.read "/f" "a\n")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 1))
  (test two_terminated (mocks (file.read "/f" "a\nb\n")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 2))
  (test two_no_final (mocks (file.read "/f" "a\nb")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 2))
  (test just_newline (mocks (file.read "/f" "\n")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 1))
  (test double_nl (mocks (file.read "/f" "\n\n")) (assert-eq (line_count "/f") 2)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Read env, uppercase. If empty, return 'NONE'.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module EnvUpDef
  (extern defun env_read (k) @capability "env.get")
  (defun get (k)
    (def v (env_read k))
    (if (str_eq v "") (return "NONE"))
    (str_upper v))
  (test missing (mocks (env.get "X" "")) (eq? (get "X") "NONE"))
  (test set (mocks (env.get "X" "hello")) (eq? (get "X") "HELLO")))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Selection sort in place; check first/last.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SelectionSort
  (defun ssort (a n)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i (- n 1))
      (do
        (def mn i)
        (def j (+ i 1))
        (while (< j n)
          (do
            (if (< (arr.get a j) (arr.get a mn)) (set mn j) 0)
            (set j (+ j 1))))
        (def t (arr.get a i))
        (arr.set a i (arr.get a mn))
        (arr.set a mn t)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    a)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 5) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 9) (arr.set a 3 1) (arr.set a 4 4)
    (ssort a 5)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 0) 1)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 4) 9))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Base $100, +50% if young (<25), -20% if senior (>=65).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Ins
  (defun premium (age)
    (def base 100)
    (if (< age 25) (return (* base 1.5)))
    (if (>= age 65) (return (* base 0.8)))
    base)
  (test young (near? (premium 20) 150.0 0.01))
  (test adult (near? (premium 40) 100.0 0.01))
  (test senior (near? (premium 70) 80.0 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 1 if array contains zero in first n.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HasZero
  (defun has_zero (xs n)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (if (= (arr_get xs i) 0) (return 1))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    0)
  (test yes
    (def a (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 0) (arr_set a 2 2)
    (eq? (has_zero a 3) 1))
  (test no
    (def a (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set a 0 1) (arr_set a 1 2) (arr_set a 2 3)
    (eq? (has_zero a 3) 0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

C->F, then >threshold flag, then string label.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module TempLabel
  (defun c_to_f (c) (+ (* c 1.8) 32.0))
  (defun is_hot (f) (if (> f 80.0) 1 0))
  (defun label (f) (if (= (is_hot f) 1) "HOT" "OK"))
  (defun classify_c (c) (label (c_to_f c)))
  (test cold (eq? (classify_c 10.0) "OK"))
  (test warm (eq? (classify_c 25.0) "OK"))
  (test hot (eq? (classify_c 35.0) "HOT")))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Steps for n to reach 1 via Collatz.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module CollatzN
  (defun csteps (n)
    (def x n) (def k 0)
    (while (> x 1)
      (do
        (if (= (math.mod x 2) 0) (set x (/ x 2)) (set x (+ (* 3 x) 1)))
        (set k (+ k 1))))
    k)
  (test one (eq? (csteps 1) 0))
  (test two (eq? (csteps 2) 1))
  (test seven (eq? (csteps 7) 16)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper compound: rate_per_period, total_periods, growth, future_value, round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Comp5
  (defun rate_per_period (annual_pct n)
    (/ (/ annual_pct 100) n))
  (defun total_periods (n years)
    (* n years))
  (defun growth (r periods)
    (math_pow (+ 1 r) periods))
  (defun future_value (principal g)
    (* principal g))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun compound (principal annual_pct n years)
    (def r (rate_per_period annual_pct n))
    (def p (total_periods n years))
    (def g (growth r p))
    (round2 (future_value principal g)))
  (test zero (near? (compound 1000 0 1 5) 1000.0 0.01))
  (test annual (near? (compound 1000 5 1 10) 1628.89 0.05))
  (test monthly (near? (compound 1000 10 12 5) 1645.31 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Convert binary number (passed as decimal) to decimal value.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Bin2Dec
  (defun bin_to_dec (bits)
    (def x bits)
    (def total 0)
    (def power 1)
    (while (> x 0)
      (def d (math_mod x 10))
      (if (= d 1) (set total (+ total power)))
      (set power (* power 2))
      (set x (math_floor (/ x 10))))
    total)
  (test zero (eq? (bin_to_dec 0) 0))
  (test one (eq? (bin_to_dec 1) 1))
  (test ten (eq? (bin_to_dec 10) 2))
  (test eleven (eq? (bin_to_dec 11) 3))
  (test full (eq? (bin_to_dec 1010) 10))
  (test eight (eq? (bin_to_dec 1000) 8)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return max of first n in array.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ArrMaxMin
  (defun arr_min (xs n)
    (def best (arr_get xs 0))
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (def v (arr_get xs i))
      (if (< v best) (set best v))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    best)
  (test single
    (def a (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set a 0 5)
    (eq? (arr_min a 1) 5))
  (test mid
    (def a (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set a 0 7) (arr_set a 1 2) (arr_set a 2 9) (arr_set a 3 1)
    (eq? (arr_min a 4) 1)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Pizza: base + toppings + crust + delivery + tip.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Pizza
  (defun base_pizza (size)
    (if (= size 0) (return 8))
    (if (= size 1) (return 12))
    16)
  (defun toppings_charge (count)
    (* count 1.5))
  (defun crust_addon (crust)
    (if (= crust 0) (return 0))
    2)
  (defun delivery (is_delivery)
    (if (= is_delivery 1) 4 0))
  (defun tip_amount (subtotal pct)
    (* subtotal (/ pct 100)))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun total (size toppings crust delivery_flag tip_pct)
    (def sub (+ (base_pizza size)
                (+ (toppings_charge toppings)
                   (+ (crust_addon crust) (delivery delivery_flag)))))
    (round2 (+ sub (tip_amount sub tip_pct))))
  (test small_plain (near? (total 0 0 0 0 0) 8.0 0.01))
  (test medium_loaded (near? (total 1 4 1 1 15) 27.6 0.05))
  (test large_delivery (near? (total 2 2 0 1 20) 27.6 0.05)))

Count non-empty whitespace-separated tokens after splitting by single space (no tab handling).

(module CountNonEmpty

    (def parts : (Array Str) (str_split s " "))

    (def c 0)

    (while (< i n)

        (if (str_eq (arr.get parts i) "") 0 (set c (+ c 1)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    c)

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Trial-division primality up to sqrt(n).

(module IsPrime
  (defun is_prime (n)
    (if (< n 2) (return 0))
    (if (= n 2) (return 1))
    (if (= (math_mod n 2) 0) (return 0))
    (def i 3)
    (def b (math_floor (math_sqrt n)))
    (while (<= i b)
      (if (= (math_mod n i) 0) (return 0))
      (set i (+ i 2)))
    1)
  (test two (eq? (is_prime 2) 1))
  (test three (eq? (is_prime 3) 1))
  (test four (eq? (is_prime 4) 0))
  (test seventeen (eq? (is_prime 17) 1))
  (test ninety_one (eq? (is_prime 91) 0)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 1 if any pair of distinct indices sums to target, 0 otherwise.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module TwoSum
  (defun has2 (a n target)
    (def found 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def j (+ i 1))
        (while (< j n)
          (do
            (if (= (+ (arr.get a i) (arr.get a j)) target) (set found 1) 0)
            (set j (+ j 1))))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    found)
  (test yes (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 2) (arr.set a 1 7) (arr.set a 2 11) (arr.set a 3 15)
    (assert-eq (has2 a 4 9) 1)))
  (test no (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 3))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3)
    (assert-eq (has2 a 3 100) 0))))

(module ClassPass
  (defun base_per_class (count rate)
    (* count rate))
  (defun bulk_discount (cost count)
    (if (>= count 20) (return (* cost 0.8)))
    (if (>= count 10) (return (* cost 0.9)))
    cost)
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun pass_cost (count rate)
    (round2 (bulk_discount (base_per_class count rate) count)))
  (test small (near? (pass_cost 5 20) 100.0 0.01))
  (test ten (near? (pass_cost 10 20) 180.0 0.01))
  (test twenty (near? (pass_cost 20 20) 320.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Period pricing + per-day insurance, then loyalty 15% off if loyalty=1, then tax_pct% on top. Round to 2 decimals.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module RentalLT
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))
  (defun period_base (days daily)
    (def weeks (math.floor (/ days 7.0)))
    (def rem (- days (* weeks 7.0)))
    (+ (* weeks (* 6.0 daily)) (* rem daily)))
  (defun rental_lt (days daily insurance loyalty tax_pct)
    (def base (period_base days daily))
    (def with_ins (+ base (* days insurance)))
    (def discounted (if (= loyalty 1) (* with_ins 0.85) with_ins))
    (def taxed (* discounted (+ 1.0 (/ tax_pct 100.0))))
    (round2 taxed))
  (test plain (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 370.0 0.01))
  (test eight_days (near? (rental_lt 8.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 430.0 0.01))
  (test loyalty_off (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 1 0.0) 314.5 0.01))
  (test taxed (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 10.0) 407.0 0.01)))
